user_query:
Please set up a safe, automated update plan for my local core platform and installed extensions. Read my preferences from input/preferences.json, my timezone from input/timezone.txt, and my installed extensions from input/installed_skills.json. Then create a control folder at output/auto-update/ with these files:
- output/auto-update/memory.md
- output/auto-update/core.md
- output/auto-update/skills.md
- output/auto-update/migrations.md
- output/auto-update/schedule.md
- output/auto-update/backups.md
- output/auto-update/run-log.md
- output/auto-update/scheduler_instructions.txt

Include the following in the files:
1) Respect my new_skill_default and core_update_mode from preferences.json. In memory.md, record the global defaults (including summary style) and set status to ongoing. In core.md, capture my core update mode (auto/notify/manual), backup scope summary, and whether I want post-update feature review.
2) In skills.md, write a Defaults section reflecting the new_skill_default, then add a Tracked Skills section listing each extension from installed_skills.json with: slug, location, installed_version, auto_update (use the value per extension if present, otherwise inherit), last_backup (leave blank for now), and migration_state (clean by default unless flagged in migrations).
3) In migrations.md, add a Pending section for any item in installed_skills.json with migration_risk true. Include from_version and to_version if provided, plus one-line possible_changes. Leave a Cleared section stub.
4) In schedule.md, set Timezone to the value in input/timezone.txt, Discovery cadence and Apply cadence from preferences.json, and specify Quiet hours if given. Add an Ownership section indicating that scheduler edits require approval and a No-op behavior line. Keep this general—no environment-specific commands.
5) Create scheduler_instructions.txt with a single, clear scheduled-job message that: a) reads the control files above, b) inspects available updates, c) backs up allowed targets first, d) skips blocked items and anything with migration risk pending, e) applies only permitted updates, f) verifies basic health, and g) writes backups.md and run-log.md. Use relative paths and do not include any tool- or OS-specific commands.
6) Initialize backups.md with headers for core and skills and empty entries. Initialize run-log.md with a stub entry template.

Stick to relative paths, don’t run anything, and don’t assume any external tools. I only need the planning and policy files written under output/auto-update/.
